records.question: Who are the users named Iron Man with email ts@richest.com, at least 6662425 followers, partition ID of at least 1, who tweeted "Hello I'm Tony Stark." on or after 2018-03-09 20:00:14?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT user_profiles.uid FROM user_profiles
JOIN tweets ON user_profiles.uid = tweets.uid
WHERE tweets.text = 'Hello I''m Tony Stark.' AND user_profiles.partitionid >= 1 AND user_profiles.name = 'Iron Man' AND user_profiles.email = 'ts@richest.com' AND user_profiles.followers >= 6662425 AND tweets.createdate >= '2018-03-09 20:00:14'
records.pred_sql: select user_profiles.uid from user_profiles join tweets on user_profiles.uid = tweets.uid where user_profiles.name like '%iron man%' and user_profiles.email like '%ts@richest.com%' and user_profiles.followers >= 6662425 and user_profiles.partitionid >= 1 and tweets.text like '%hello i''m tony stark.%' and tweets.createdate >= '2018-03-09 20:00:14' limit 5;
records.thought: The query needs to filter user_profiles based on the specified name, email, followers, partition ID, and the associated tweets that match the given text and date criteria. A join between user_profiles and tweets is necessary to access the tweet information.

records.question: Which user profiles have the name Susan?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT user_profiles.uid FROM user_profiles
WHERE user_profiles.name = 'Susan'
records.pred_sql: select user_profiles.uid from user_profiles where name like '%susan%' limit 5
records.thought: The query needs to find user profiles with the name 'Susan', so it will filter the user_profiles table based on the name column and select the uid.

records.question: Which user profiles have the email ts@richest.com and the name Iron Man, with at least partitionid 1 and exactly 6662425 followers, who tweeted "My company is hiring interns." on 2018-03-12 02:30:30?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT user_profiles.uid FROM user_profiles
JOIN tweets ON user_profiles.uid = tweets.uid
WHERE user_profiles.email = 'ts@richest.com' AND user_profiles.name = 'Iron Man' AND tweets.text = 'My company is hiring interns.' AND user_profiles.partitionid >= 1 AND tweets.createdate = '2018-03-12 02:30:30' AND user_profiles.followers = 6662425
records.pred_sql: select user_profiles.uid from user_profiles join tweets on user_profiles.uid = tweets.uid where user_profiles.email = 'ts@richest.com' and user_profiles.name like '%iron man%' and user_profiles.partitionid >= 1 and user_profiles.followers = 6662425 and tweets.text like '%my company is hiring interns%' and tweets.createdate = '2018-03-12 02:30:30' limit 5;
records.thought: The query needs to filter user profiles based on specific email, name, partitionid, followers count, and a specific tweet text and date. This requires joining the user_profiles table with the tweets table to find matching tweets.

records.question: Which user profiles have a partition ID of at most 5, an email of np@superstar.com, at least 94375428 followers, and a name exactly Natalie Portman?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT user_profiles.uid FROM user_profiles
WHERE user_profiles.partitionid <= 5 AND user_profiles.email = 'np@superstar.com' AND user_profiles.followers >= 94375428 AND user_profiles.name = 'Natalie Portman'
records.pred_sql: select user_profiles.uid from user_profiles where user_profiles.partitionid <= 5 and user_profiles.email = 'np@superstar.com' and user_profiles.followers >= 94375428 and user_profiles.name = 'Natalie Portman' limit 5;
records.thought: The query needs to filter user profiles based on specific criteria including partition ID, email, followers, and name. Since we only need to select from the user_profiles table, no joins are necessary.

records.question: Who are the users with the email cd@superstar.com that have posted tweets?

WHERE user_profiles.email = 'cd@superstar.com'
records.pred_sql: select user_profiles.uid from user_profiles join tweets on user_profiles.uid = tweets.uid where user_profiles.email = 'cd@superstar.com' limit 5;

records.question: Which user profiles have at least 23927492 followers, belong to partition 5, have the email cd@superstar.com, and tweeted exactly "I had a pretty rough time during the last couple of weeks"?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT user_profiles.uid FROM user_profiles
JOIN tweets ON user_profiles.uid = tweets.uid
WHERE user_profiles.followers >= 23927492 AND user_profiles.partitionid = 5 AND tweets.text = 'I had a pretty rough time during the last couple of weeks' AND user_profiles.email = 'cd@superstar.com'
records.pred_sql: select user_profiles.uid from user_profiles join tweets on user_profiles.uid = tweets.uid where user_profiles.followers >= 23927492 and user_profiles.partitionid = 5 and user_profiles.email = 'cd@superstar.com' and tweets.text = 'I had a pretty rough time during the last couple of weeks' limit 5;
records.thought: The query needs to filter user profiles based on the specified criteria, including followers count, partition ID, email, and a specific tweet text. A join with the tweets table is necessary to check the tweet content.
